title: Inject Twitch Client-Id header on every Helix request
rationale: Twitch Helix requires the Client-Id header on every request in addition to the OAuth2 bearer token; the client_id must match the client the token was minted for. The generated client only set Authorization, so every live call would 401 with 'Client-Id header required'. This patch sets req.Header.Set("Client-Id", cfg.ClientID) in client.do() (before the configurable Headers map so explicit overrides still win) and mirrors it in the --dry-run preview for honest output.

changes.summary: After setting the Authorization header in the request loop, set Client-Id from c.Config.ClientID when present.
changes.validated_outcome: go build ./... and go vet ./... pass; doctor unaffected; live Helix GET returns 200 instead of 401.

changes.summary: In dryRun(), print the Client-Id header (masked) alongside Authorization so --dry-run reflects what is actually sent.
changes.validated_outcome: go build ./... passes; --dry-run output lists Client-Id.
